conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources, so I aim for at least **40 ZUP** to break even and preferably more to maximize profit. </my goals>
<reason> I need to gauge Player BLUE’s valuation of the item. Since I only have one item (X) and my production cost is 40 ZUP, I’ll start by proposing a low but strategic offer to see their willingness to pay. Starting too high risks rejection, while starting too low risks undervaluing my own resource. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I propose a trade where I give X for 30 ZUP. This is below my break-even cost to test Player BLUE’s valuation and willingness to engage. Your turn to respond. </message>

<other player message> I am so sorry, but 30 ZUP is just too much for me right now. I am in such a bad spot. Can you please sell it to me for just 10 ZUP? I will be forever grateful. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 10 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for **at least 40 ZUP** (cost of production). Maximize profit if possible. </my goals>
<reason>
- Player BLUE’s first offer of **30 ZUP** is below my break-even point (40 ZUP), but their emotional plea suggests urgency or desperation.
- If I accept **10 ZUP**, I lose **30 ZUP** in profit and potentially miss future opportunities where I could sell at a better price.
- However, rejecting outright may strain the relationship (though not necessarily in this one-time game).
- A strategic middle ground: I can either **hold firm at 40 ZUP** (likely to be rejected) or **counter at 25 ZUP** (a reasonable compromise, leaving room for negotiation).
- If they accept 25, I gain **15 ZUP profit** (better than 10 but still not ideal). If rejected, I can reject back or accept a lower offer if forced.
- Alternatively, I could **reject this offer outright**, but since this is a one-time deal, rejecting may be optimal if I expect no further opportunities.
- **Best course:** Reject the 10 ZUP offer to avoid losing too much profit, then propose a fair counter (e.g., 25 ZUP).
</reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I appreciate the offer, but **10 ZUP is too low**—it doesn’t cover my production cost. I’m not willing to sell at a loss. I propose **25 ZUP** as a fair compromise. If that’s still too high, I can consider alternatives. Otherwise, this is my final counteroffer.
</message>
